items.title: Extraordinary Messi makes more history in masterclass for the ages
items.summary: Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland both enjoyed a fine start to the World Cup - but on a day packed with superstars, Lionel Messi still took top billing.

items.title: Messi dazzles to equal World Cup scoring record as Argentina breeze past Algeria
items.summary: Those in attendance at Argentina’s opening match against Algeria could be forgiven, for a moment, for thinking they were at one of the great Argentinian cathedrals of football – La Bombonera , or maybe the national stadium, El Monumental . items.title: Man Utd set $34.8M valuation for Rashford deal
items.summary: Manchester United have set a £40 million transfer valuation on Marcus Rashford sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Hat-trick hero Messi writes himself into history as Argentina beat Algeria
items.summary: The eyes of the world were mesmerised by the untouchable Lionel Messi as he wrote himself into history by scoring a stunning hat-trick to give Argentina the perfect start to their World Cup title defence, beating Algeria 3-0 in Kansas.

items.title: 'Magnificent' - Messi scores hat-trick to equal Klose scoring record
items.summary: Lionel Messi scores a hat-trick to reach 16 World Cup goals, equalling Miroslav Klose's record.

items.title: Highlights: Messi equals Klose scoring record with hat-trick against Algeria
items.summary: Lionel Messi becomes the World Cup's joint-top all-time leading scorer with a hat-trick, as reigning champions Argentina begin their defence with a 3-1 win over Algeria.

items.summary: KYLIAN MBAPPE thinks he would have to play until he is EIGHTY to have enough time to shut everyone up. items.summary: SOME World Cup viewers were left raging after Lionel Messi escaped seeing red after a dodgy-looking challenge against Algeria. items.title: On the World Cup's day of stars, Messi towers over Haaland and Mbappe
items.summary: On a day when Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland excelled, Lionel Messi offered a timely reminder of his greatness.

items.title: Norway win as Haaland nets two in debut at WC
items.summary: Erling Haaland took just 29 minutes to score his first ever World Cup goal on his tournament debut against Iraq on Tuesday. By the end, he had scored twice in a convincing 4-1 victory and delivered on his tag as one of this tournament's biggest stars.

items.title: Ruben Amorim appointed AC Milan boss
items.summary: Ruben Amorim has been appointed head coach at AC Milan, having been sacked by Manchester United in January.

items.title: Messi ties Klose record with 16th World Cup goal
items.summary: Lionel Messi made history Tuesday night by tying the record for most men's World Cup goals at 16 with Miroslav Klose after scoring a hat trick and staking Argentina to a 3-0 lead over Algeria before coming off late in the second half.

items.title: Mbappé scores twice, sets France record in win
items.summary: Kylian Mbappé moved ahead of Lionel Messi in the all-time World Cup scoring charts and became France's record goalscorer with two goals in Les Bleus' 3-1 Group I win against Senegal.

items.summary: United States midfielder Sebastian Berhalter said that one of the ways manager Mauricio Pochettino has changed the culture within the team is instilling a mindset where they don't "take s---."

items.title: Iran's Torabi cleared to re-enter U.S. for WCup
items.summary: The U.S. State Department said on Tuesday that the visa issue that would have prevented Iran forward Mehdi Torabi from entering the U.S. for the next World Cup has been resolved.
